title: I skipped college and now make over $40/hr building data centers
excerpt: Cody Cole chose to enter the trades after high school rather than going to college. Now, he makes over $40 an hour working on data centers.
content_html:
This as-told-to essay is based on a conversation with Cody Cole, 25, an Arizona-based electrical field superintendent with Corbins, an industrial electrical contractor. The following has been edited for length and clarity.

I started in the electrical field in 2019 at 18 years old, shortly after getting out of high school. At first, I wasn't particularly thrilled about doing electrical work. But my brother, dad, uncle, and grandpa all worked with their hands, and I'm very similar to them, so I thought I'd give it a try.

As I immersed myself more and saw all the positive career opportunities that arose, I found that I began to like my role.

Now, seven years later, I'm a superintendent making over $40 an hour with 45 people who report to me. It's helped relieve the financial stress my wife and I have from everyday life, as we raise three kids and give them the best upbringing we can.

I didn't want to pay for a traditional education

I've never been amazing at school; it wasn't something I looked forward to. My parents taught me to pay my own way and earn my own money — something I greatly appreciate — so paying for a traditional college education wasn't something I wanted to do.

With a career in industrial electrical work, I knew I was able to start making good money right out of high school.

The AI boom also changed my perspective on my career in electrical. I've seen firsthand that there's going to continue to be a need for skilled tradesmen; at my company, Corbins, we specialize in data centers, which are going to be needed with the expansion of AI.

After entering the industry without any formal training, one of the turning points of my career came a year and a half in, when I had the opportunity to do an electrical apprenticeship program through the Western Electrical Contractors Association (WECA).

Corbins would fund my schooling and pay my salary while I attended classes for two-week blocks every three to six months, across four years. The program consisted of a minimum of 156 hours of classroom instruction a year and 8,000 hours of on-the-job training.

I couldn't justify not going to school — it was such a great opportunity. I ended up graduating as salutatorian in 2024.

My salary grew from $15 an hour to over $40 an hour

I had started out making $15 an hour — which was awesome for not having any experience — but I've progressed quickly from doing hands-on labor to being a foreman to being a superintendent. Now my hourly rate is in the high $40s.

I could've even started making more, faster, if I'd jumped into the apprenticeship sooner.

I don't feel I'd be in the financial position to be the sole provider of my family, had I chosen a different path. We've been fortunate that with my income, we've been able to achieve our goal of my wife being a full-time stay-at-home mom.

There's so much opportunity for young people. One of the foremen who works with me is only 21, and he's phenomenal at his job. Another superintendent I work with is also amazing, and he's only 23 or 24.

The data center boom means endless job opportunities

There's a lot of job security in what we do. With the expansion of AI going at an extreme rate, there's plenty of work all over the country with these jobs. The opportunities are endless — not only on the construction side of things, with physical, hands-on labor, but also with the other positions it takes to build these data centers, such as accounting, financing, and planning.

For the last two years, I've been working fully on data center projects. I've been fortunate enough to travel to New Mexico a few times and to Texas; the data centers are going up all over the place, so the work is there.

I know there are controversies around data centers. I'm not an expert, but I believe the ones I'm working on are quiet and not intrusive. They're also very necessary for the way technology is going, in order for AI to progress, and are providing new work opportunities, like the ones I've had.

Although I've only been doing this for seven years, I enjoy mentoring others

Although I'm now a superintendent, I still feel like I'm new to the trades and there's still a ton that I want to learn. However, I'm enjoying being able to mentor others and see the bigger picture of the projects we're working on.

When I was learning, people taught me the "why" behind what I was doing; that's something I'm now able to share with the people I work with, especially as the AI industry and data centers are fairly new.

In my role, I plan for the whole building, but in the higher positions, they're looking at an even larger picture of the impact and what's coming next. Understanding the big picture of projects is an aspect of my career I really enjoy and am looking forward to continuing.

I don't want to do something just because I'm told to — I want to know why I'm doing it. It makes the work more meaningful, and makes me want to try harder, give the job my full effort, and train those around me so they can learn from me too.

For the people I manage, and for people who are new to the industry, data center construction gives them an opportunity to get into the industry as it's booming, learn, and progress their careers quickly.

Do you have a story to share about pursuing a blue-collar career rather than a corporate one? Contact this editor, Jane Zhang, at janezhang@businessinsider.com.

Read next

Jamie Phillis is a Phoenix native and Arizona State University graduate who specializes in lifestyle and features writing. You can usually find her traveling the globe, at the spin studio or a concert, or trying a new restaurant. Follow her on Instagram at @jamiefayekillin, and see more of her work on her website.

  • Data Centers

sentences.zh: 科迪·科尔 (Cody Cole) 是一名 25 岁的电气现场主管，没有大学学位，时薪超过 40 美元。

sentences.en: Courtesy of Nox Group
sentences.zh: 由诺克斯集团提供

blocks.html_en: This as-told-to essay is based on a conversation with Cody Cole, 25, an Arizona-based electrical field superintendent with Corbins, an industrial electrical contractor. The following has been edited for length and clarity.

sentences.en: The following has been edited for length and clarity.
sentences.zh: 为了篇幅和清晰度，以下内容已被编辑。

blocks.html_en: I started in the electrical field in 2019 at 18 years old, shortly after getting out of high school. At first, I wasn't particularly thrilled about doing electrical work. But my brother, dad, uncle, and grandpa all worked with their hands, and I'm very similar to them, so I thought I'd give it a try.

sentences.en: But my brother, dad, uncle, and grandpa all worked with their hands, and I'm very similar to them, so I thought I'd give it a try.
sentences.zh: 但我的哥哥、爸爸、叔叔、爷爷都是靠双手干活的，我和他们很相似，所以我想我应该尝试一下。

blocks.html_en: As I immersed myself more and saw all the positive career opportunities that arose, I found that I began to like my role.

sentences.en: As I immersed myself more and saw all the positive career opportunities that arose, I found that I began to like my role.
sentences.zh: 当我更加沉浸并看到出现的所有积极的职业机会时，我发现我开始喜欢我的角色。

blocks.html_en: Now, seven years later, I'm a superintendent making over $40 an hour with 45 people who report to me. It's helped relieve the financial stress my wife and I have from everyday life, as we raise three kids and give them the best upbringing we can.

sentences.en: Now, seven years later, I'm a superintendent making over $40 an hour with 45 people who report to me.
sentences.zh: 七年后的现在，我是一名主管，时薪超过 40 美元，手下有 45 名向我汇报的人。

sentences.en: It's helped relieve the financial stress my wife and I have from everyday life, as we raise three kids and give them the best upbringing we can.
sentences.zh: 它帮助缓解了我和妻子日常生活中的经济压力，因为我们抚养了三个孩子，并为他们提供了最好的教育。

blocks.html_en: I didn't want to pay for a traditional education

sentences.en: My parents taught me to pay my own way and earn my own money — something I greatly appreciate — so paying for a traditional college education wasn't something I wanted to do.
sentences.zh: 我的父母教导我要自己付钱，自己挣钱——这是我非常感激的——所以支付传统的大学教育费用并不是我想做的事情。

blocks.html_en: With a career in industrial electrical work, I knew I was able to start making good money right out of high school.

sentences.en: With a career in industrial electrical work, I knew I was able to start making good money right out of high school.
sentences.zh: 由于从事工业电气工作，我知道我高中毕业后就能开始赚大钱。

blocks.html_en: The AI boom also changed my perspective on my career in electrical. I've seen firsthand that there's going to continue to be a need for skilled tradesmen; at my company, Corbins, we specialize in data centers, which are going to be needed with the expansion of AI.

sentences.en: The AI boom also changed my perspective on my career in electrical.
sentences.zh: 人工智能的繁荣也改变了我对电气职业的看法。

sentences.en: I've seen firsthand that there's going to continue to be a need for skilled tradesmen; at my company, Corbins, we specialize in data centers, which are going to be needed with the expansion of AI.
sentences.zh: 我亲眼目睹了对熟练技工的需求仍然存在。在我的公司 Corbins，我们专注于数据中心，这是随着人工智能的扩展而需要的。

blocks.html_en: After entering the industry without any formal training, one of the turning points of my career came a year and a half in, when I had the opportunity to do an electrical apprenticeship program through the Western Electrical Contractors Association (WECA).

sentences.en: After entering the industry without any formal training, one of the turning points of my career came a year and a half in, when I had the opportunity to do an electrical apprenticeship program through the Western Electrical Contractors Association (WECA).
sentences.zh: 在没有接受任何正式培训的情况下进入该行业后，我职业生涯的转折点之一出现在一年半后，当时我有机会通过西部电气承包商协会 (WECA) 进行电气学徒计划。

blocks.html_en: Corbins would fund my schooling and pay my salary while I attended classes for two-week blocks every three to six months, across four years. The program consisted of a minimum of 156 hours of classroom instruction a year and 8,000 hours of on-the-job training.

sentences.en: The program consisted of a minimum of 156 hours of classroom instruction a year and 8,000 hours of on-the-job training.
sentences.zh: 该计划包括每年至少 156 小时的课堂教学和 8,000 小时的在职培训。

sentences.en: Cody Cole participated in an electrical apprenticeship program alongside his job.
sentences.zh: 科迪·科尔 (Cody Cole) 在工作之余还参加了电气学徒计划。

sentences.en: Now my hourly rate is in the high $40s.

sentences.zh: 如果我早点开始学徒生涯，我什至可以开始做得更多、更快。

blocks.html_en: I don't feel I'd be in the financial position to be the sole provider of my family, had I chosen a different path. We've been fortunate that with my income, we've been able to achieve our goal of my wife being a full-time stay-at-home mom.

sentences.en: We've been fortunate that with my income, we've been able to achieve our goal of my wife being a full-time stay-at-home mom.
sentences.zh: 我们很幸运，凭借我的收入，我们能够实现我妻子成为全职妈妈的目标。

sentences.zh: 年轻人的机会太多了。

sentences.zh: 与我一起工作的另一位主管也很棒，他只有 23 或 24 岁。

sentences.en: In my role, I plan for the whole building, but in the higher positions, they're looking at an even larger picture of the impact and what's coming next.
sentences.zh: 在我的角色中，我为整个建筑进行规划，但在更高的职位上，他们会着眼于更大的影响以及接下来会发生什么。

sentences.en: Understanding the big picture of projects is an aspect of my career I really enjoy and am looking forward to continuing.
sentences.zh: 了解项目的大局是我职业生涯的一个方面，我非常喜欢并期待继续下去。

sentences.zh: 我不想仅仅因为别人告诉我去做某事——我想知道为什么我要这么做。

sentences.zh: 它让工作变得更有意义，让我想要更加努力，全力以赴，培训我周围的人，让他们也能向我学习。

blocks.html_en: For the people I manage, and for people who are new to the industry, data center construction gives them an opportunity to get into the industry as it's booming, learn, and progress their careers quickly.

blocks.html_en: • as told to

sentences.en: as told to
sentences.zh: 正如所告知的

blocks.html_en: • Data Centers

sentences.en: Data Centers
sentences.zh: 数据中心

content_html_zh:
这篇众所周知的文章基于与 25 岁的亚利桑那州电力现场主管科迪·科尔 (Cody Cole) 和工业电气承包商科尔宾斯 (Corbins) 的对话。 为了篇幅和清晰度，以下内容已被编辑。

2019 年，我 18 岁，高中毕业后不久，就开始涉足电气领域。 起初，我对做电气工作并不是特别感兴趣。 但我的哥哥、爸爸、叔叔、爷爷都是靠双手干活的，我和他们很相似，所以我想我应该尝试一下。

当我更加沉浸并看到出现的所有积极的职业机会时，我发现我开始喜欢我的角色。

七年后的现在，我是一名主管，时薪超过 40 美元，手下有 45 名向我汇报的人。 它帮助缓解了我和妻子日常生活中的经济压力，因为我们抚养了三个孩子，并为他们提供了最好的教育。

我不想支付传统教育的费用

我在学校从来没有表现得很出色；这不是我所期待的。 我的父母教导我要自己付钱，自己挣钱——这是我非常感激的——所以支付传统的大学教育费用并不是我想做的事情。

由于从事工业电气工作，我知道我高中毕业后就能开始赚大钱。

人工智能的繁荣也改变了我对电气职业的看法。 我亲眼目睹了对熟练技工的需求仍然存在。在我的公司 Corbins，我们专注于数据中心，这是随着人工智能的扩展而需要的。

在没有接受任何正式培训的情况下进入该行业后，我职业生涯的转折点之一出现在一年半后，当时我有机会通过西部电气承包商协会 (WECA) 进行电气学徒计划。

四年来，科尔宾斯会资助我的学费并支付我的工资，而我每三到六个月就参加为期两周的课程。 该计划包括每年至少 156 小时的课堂教学和 8,000 小时的在职培训。

我没有理由不去上学——这是一个很好的机会。 我最终于 2024 年以致辞者身份毕业。

我的工资从每小时 15 美元增加到每小时 40 美元以上

一开始我每小时挣 15 美元——对于没有任何经验来说这真是太棒了——但我已经从做手工劳动迅速进步到成为一名工头，再成为一名主管。 现在我的时薪高达 40 美元。

如果我早点开始学徒生涯，我什至可以开始做得更多、更快。

如果我选择不同的道路，我觉得我的经济状况不会成为家庭唯一的经济来源。 我们很幸运，凭借我的收入，我们能够实现我妻子成为全职妈妈的目标。

年轻人的机会太多了。 和我一起工作的一位工头只有 21 岁，但他的工作表现非常出色。 与我一起工作的另一位主管也很棒，他只有 23 或 24 岁。

数据中心的繁荣意味着无限的就业机会

我们所做的工作有很多工作保障。 随着人工智能的飞速发展，全国各地都有大量的此类工作。 机会是无穷无尽的——不仅在建筑方面，包括体力劳动，而且还包括建设这些数据中心所需的其他职位，例如会计、财务和规划。

在过去的两年里，我一直致力于数据中心项目。 我很幸运曾多次前往新墨西哥州和德克萨斯州。各地都在建设数据中心，所以工作就在那里。

我知道围绕数据中心存在争议。 我不是专家，但我相信我正在研究的内容是安静且不具有侵入性的。 它们对于技术的发展、人工智能的进步也是非常必要的，并且提供了新的工作机会，就像我所拥有的那样。

虽然我只做了七年，但我喜欢指导别人

虽然我现在是一名主管，但我仍然觉得自己是这个行业的新手，还有很多东西我想学习。 然而，我很高兴能够指导他人并了解我们正在开展的项目的整体情况。

当我学习时，人们告诉我我所做的事情背后的“原因”；我现在可以与我的同事分享这一点，特别是在人工智能行业和数据中心还相当新的情况下。

在我的角色中，我为整个建筑进行规划，但在更高的职位上，他们会着眼于更大的影响以及接下来会发生什么。 了解项目的大局是我职业生涯的一个方面，我非常喜欢并期待继续下去。

我不想仅仅因为别人告诉我去做某事——我想知道为什么我要这么做。 它让工作变得更有意义，让我想要更加努力，全力以赴，培训我周围的人，让他们也能向我学习。

对于我管理的人员，以及刚进入这个行业的人来说，数据中心建设让他们有机会进入这个正在蓬勃发展的行业，学习并快速发展自己的职业生涯。

关于追求蓝领职业而不是企业职业，您有什么故事可以分享吗？ 请联系本编辑 Jane 张，邮箱为 janezhang@businessinsider.com。

阅读下一篇

杰米·菲利斯 (Jamie Phillis) 是凤凰城本地人，毕业于亚利桑那州立大学，擅长生活方式和写作。 你通常可以看到她环游世界，在旋转工作室或音乐会上，或者尝试一家新餐厅。 在 Instagram 上关注她：@jamiefayekillin，并在她的网站上查看更多她的作品。
